latest.title: Publication of Phase 1 Clinical Trial
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4746H
Imaging Biometrics Limited
09 June 2026
Imaging Biometrics Limited
("Company")
9 June 2026
Publication of Phase 1 Clinical Trial of Oral Gallium Maltolate (GaM) in Recurrent Glioblastoma and Reopening of Expanded Access Programme
Imaging Biometrics Limited (LSE: IBAI) announces the publication in the peer-reviewed journal
Neuro-Oncology Advances
describing the results from the Phase 1 clinical trial (NCT04319276) of oral gallium maltolate in patients with recurrent or treatment-refractory glioblastoma (GBM). Imaging Biometrics, LLC, a wholly owned subsidiary of Imaging Biometrics Limited, was the primary sponsor of the trial.
This marks the first in-human study results of GaM for glioblastoma.
The trial, conducted at the Froedtert and Medical College of Wisconsin Clinical Cancer Center, demonstrated that oral GaM was well tolerated and showed preliminary signals of survival benefit, supporting further clinical development.
Summary of published results
The trial was a dose-escalation study designed primarily to assess safety and to establish a recommended Phase 2 dose (RP2D). Key findings reported by the investigators were:
• Oral gallium maltolate was safe and well tolerated at all dose levels up to 2,500 mg/day. Only Grade 1 or Grade 2 adverse effects were observed, with no Grade 3 or higher toxicities. No kidney toxicity was seen. The absence of nephrotoxicity is particularly notable given historical renal risks associated with earlier gallium formulations and supports the feasibility of chronic oral administration in patients with recurrent glioblastoma.
• The recommended Phase 2 dose (RP2D) was determined to be 2,000 mg/day.
• Among the 22 patients evaluable for tumour response, the investigators reported a median overall survival of 16 months and a median progression-free survival of 2.5 months. The authors note that the median overall survival compares favourably with the range of 5.5 to 12.6 months reported in a published meta-analysis of treatments for recurrent GBM, while emphasising that no conclusions on efficacy can be drawn from a Phase 1 study of this design.
• Of note, 13 of 22 patients achieved a survival, measured from start of treatment with GaM, of greater than 12 months. One patient achieved a partial response and remains on treatment after more than 38 months.
The authors conclude that further investigation of the clinical efficacy of gallium maltolate in GBM is warranted, and that this will require a statistically powered, Phase 2 trial.
Regulatory status
Gallium maltolate holds Fast Track, two Orphan Drug and two Rare Pediatric Disease designations from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA). These designations relate to the regulatory development pathway and do not constitute any determination of efficacy by the FDA.
Reopening of the Expanded Access Programme
In response to continued interest from patients and clinicians, Imaging Biometrics intends to reactivate its Expanded Access Programme (EAP) to enrol up to 20 additional patients. The EAP provides a route for eligible patients to access investigational gallium maltolate outside of a clinical trial. As an investigational agent, GaM has not been approved by any regulatory authority; its safety and efficacy have not been established. Decisions regarding suitability for the EAP are a matter for a patient's treating physician.
Comment
Trevor Brown, Chief Executive Officer of Imaging Biometrics Ltd, commented:
"We are encouraged that this trial has established a safe dosing regimen and a recommended Phase 2 dose for oral gallium maltolate. Although the study was not powered for efficacy, the results provide encouraging evidence of biological and clinical activity, including durable disease stabilization and prolonged survival in select patients. We are grateful to the patients and clinical investigators who made it possible. Glioblastoma remains an area of profound unmet need."
For further information, please contact:
Imaging Biometrics Limited
Trevor Brown / Brett Skelly / Michael Schmainda / John Richardson
Tel: 020 7469 0930
AlbR Capital Limited
Corporate Broker

latest.title: Commercialisation, Phase II Results & Revenue Path
latest.content:
5 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 0999H
Imaging Biometrics Limited
05 June 2026
IMAGING BIOMETRICS LIMITED
("IBAI" or the "Company")
(LSE: IBAI)
5 June 2026
IB Neuro™ Commercialisation Plan - ASCO 2026 Phase II Results and Strategic Revenue Pathway
Imaging Biometrics Limited (LSE: IBAI) is pleased to update shareholders on the EAF151 Phase II clinical trial results presented at the American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO) Annual Meeting on 31 May 2026, and to outline the Board's plans to drive revenue growth from its IB Neuro platform.
EAF151 is a prospective, multicentre Phase II study conducted across 33 centres and 146 patients, sponsored by ECOG-ACRIN, part of the US National Clinical Trials Network (NCTN). The study evaluated whether dynamic susceptibility contrast (DSC) MRI-derived cerebral blood volume, computed using IB Neuro, could provide an early and objective biomarker of response to bevacizumab in recurrent glioblastoma (rGBM).
Centralized image processing was performed by the ACRIN core lab. IB Delta T1 (dT1) maps were generated from standard MRI scans for all examinations to objectively delineate true regions of contrast enhancement. IB Neuro was used to compute both normalized and standardized relative cerebral blood volume (nRCBV and sRCBV, respectively). Mean nRCBV and sRCBV values were then calculated within the dT1-defined contrast-enhancing regions.
Both baseline and early (2 week) post-treatment rCBV measurements-whether derived using conventional manual normalization or automated standardized methods-demonstrated meaningful associations with patient outcomes. Notably, IB Neuro's sRCBV, a fully automated and operator-independent output map, unique to IB, showed the strongest statistically significant association with overall survival (OS), outperforming manually normalized rCBV. These findings position EAF151 as one of the first prospective, multicenter trials to validate an automated advanced imaging biomarker for OS prediction in recurrent glioblastoma.
Because bevacizumab is costly and does not benefit all patients, an objective early-response signal has material clinical and economic value: it could help clinicians determine within weeks rather than months whether treatment is working, reducing exposure to ineffective therapy, limiting unnecessary cost, and facilitating earlier transition to alternative treatments. The Board believes this positions IB Neuro as a significant clinical decision-support tool in an under-served and high-cost disease area.
The Group's commercialisation strategy targets both participating EAF151 sites and organizations conducting clinical trials with anti-angiogenic agents-including clinical research organizations (CROs) and trial sponsors-through a combination of direct sales channels and global distribution partners. Early response assessment offers a compelling and immediate value proposition for trial sponsors and clinicians, where timely and data-driven decision-making is critical. The EAF151 results provide prospective clinical validation of this approach, positioning IB Neuro's sRCBV as an operationally practical and readily deployable solution in comparable trial settings and for routine clinical use. Collectively, these opportunities underpin a capital-efficient strategy focused on generating incremental revenue from existing infrastructure without the need for material external funding.
Michael Schmainda, Chief Executive Officer of Imaging Biometrics, LLC (the Group's US operating subsidiary), commented: "The EAF151 results show that the technology built in IB Neuro can provide early, objective signals of treatment response-within weeks rather than months-offering the potential to streamline clinical trials and help clinicians everywhere identify more effective treatment strategies sooner."
For further information:
Imaging Biometrics Limited
Trevor Brown / Brett Skelly / Michael Schmainda / John Richardson
Tel: 020 7469 0930
AlbR Capital Limited
Corporate
Broker
Lucy Williams / Duncan Vasey
Tel: 020 7
469 0930
About Imaging Biometrics® LLC
IB is a wholly owned subsidiary of Imaging Biometrics Limited (LSE: IBAI) and a world leader in quantitative neuroimaging software for the assessment and monitoring of brain tumours. Its FDA-cleared, UKCA- and CE-marked platform is distributed globally through GE HealthCare, Blackford Analysis, aycan Medical Systems, and Prism Clinical Imaging. For more information, visit www.imagingbiometrics.com.

latest.title: AGM Statement and Business Update
latest.content:
3 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 5405G
Imaging Biometrics Limited
03 June 2026
IMAGING BIOMETRICS LIMITED
("IBAI" or the "Company")
(LSE: IBAI)
AGM Statement and Business Update
3 June 2026
Imaging Biometrics Limited (LSE: IBAI) is pleased to provide this update to shareholders
ahead of
its Annual General Meeting
being
held today. The Group develops quantitative neuroimaging software and organ-on-a-chip technology for healthcare and life sciences.
IB Nimble™ 2 - Commercial Launch
The Company today announces the release of IB Nimble™ 2, a substantially upgraded version of its secure mobile and web-based platform for multidisciplinary clinical collaboration. It is available on web, iOS, and Android. It is designed to support tumour board discussions, complex disease management workflows, and emergent care settings.
The release incorporates material enhancements developed in direct response to feedback from existing clinical users. In-app DICOM (medical image format) viewing allows care teams to review imaging within the platform without switching between systems. PACS (picture archiving and communication system) synchronisation makes imaging data accessible in clinical context without local installation. The release also adds customisable roles and permissions, synchronised cross-platform experience, and reinforced cybersecurity architecture.
IB Nimble™ 2 is available immediately to both existing and prospective customers. The Board expects the release to broaden the platform's addressability to institutions not previously able to justify adoption without integrated imaging capability.
IB Nimble™ 2 forms part of the Company's broader strategy to sharpen its focus on its neuroimaging software portfolio, prioritising high-quality, commercially sustainable products, as outlined in the strategic review published in February 2026.
"The ability to sync with PACS and view DICOM images within IB Nimble™ 2 fundamentally changes how clinicians and researchers collaborate. Users can now review imaging data in context, alongside colleagues, in real time - without switching between systems. That is a meaningful improvement in both efficiency and clinical decision-making."
- Dr Joseph Bovi MD, Physician Chief, ThedaCare Cancer Service Line
"IB Nimble™ 2 is the direct product of listening to the clinicians who use our platform every day. Integrated image viewing within a mobile collaboration tool removes a genuine friction point in tumour board workflows. We are pleased to deliver this to our user base and to make it available to new institutions as we continue to build on the commercial momentum established in 2025."
- Trevor Brown, Chief Executive, Imaging Biometrics Limited
QSMetric™ - GE HealthCare Distribution
In December 2025, the Company's distribution relationship with GE HealthCare was expanded. The expanded agreement now covers QSMetric™ and FTB Express Maps alongside the existing IB Neuro® and IB Delta T1™ products. This gives IBAI's software access to GE's installed base of MRI systems worldwide.
QSMetric™ is a patented, FDA-cleared quantitative susceptibility mapping (QSM) solution developed and validated at Weill Cornell Medicine. QSMetric™ detects and measures susceptibility-based biomarkers-particularly iron and microhemorrhages-supporting diagnosis, monitoring, and treatment planning across a range of neurological conditions including amyloid-related imaging abnormalities - hemorrhagic (ARIA-H) in patients receiving anti-amyloid Alzheimer's therapies. FDA-mandated ARIA monitoring is a protocol requirement for these treatments.
IBAI contributes its certified Quality Management System, regulatory expertise, and global support infrastructure. Integration of QSMetric™ into GE's distribution ecosystem is progressing, and the Board views this as an incremental revenue opportunity for the Group.
Kirkstall Limited - Organ-on-a-Chip Platform
Kirkstall, acquired in October 2025, delivered year-on-year revenue growth in 2025 and has a distributor network spanning the United States, China, and South Korea. New distribution partners have been added in recent months, further expanding commercial reach.
The Quasi-Vivo® organ-on-a-chip platform is in active use in pharmaceutical research, toxicology, and disease modelling. A BBSRC/NC3Rs-funded collaboration with Nottingham Trent University is developing a glioblastoma tumour-on-a-chip model. This opens a route into pharmaceutical drug discovery, a segment with larger and recurring order volumes.
Kirkstall is transitioning from a university-centric business toward a commercially scalable operation. The platform is well positioned to benefit from the global regulatory shift away from animal testing, including the US FDA Modernization Act 2.0 and equivalent mandates in the UK, EU, China and South Korea.
Gallium Maltolate - Phase 1 Publication
Following the successful completion of the Phase 1 clinical trial of oral gallium maltolate (GaM) in November 2025, preparation of the study results for journal submission is continuing. The Board expects submission in 2026 and will update shareholders once it has been made. The Group retains contractual rights to the Phase 1 data for regulatory and commercialisation purposes.
Chief Executive's Statement
"Imaging Biometrics enters this AGM with commercial momentum that is genuine and measurable. Our neuroimaging software is now distributed by global partners. Our mobile collaboration platform has been substantially upgraded. Our organ-on-a-chip business is generating early commercial traction. With IB Nimble™ 2 launched and the enhanced IB Clinic platform nearing release, new Kirkstall distribution partners in place, and global distribution relationships expanded, the Board is confident the Group is entering a period of revenue acceleration. Our expectation of progress towards profitability in 2026 is unchanged."
- Trevor Brown, Chief Executive Officer, Imaging Biometrics Limited
- ENDS -
Imaging Biometrics Limited
Trevor Brown / Brett Skelly / Michael Schmainda / John Richardson
Tel: 020 7469 0930
AlbR Capital Limited
Corporate
Broker
Lucy Williams
Tel: 020 7
4690930
About Imaging Biometrics Limited
Imaging Biometrics Limited (LSE: IBAI) is a healthcare technology group with two principal subsidiaries. Imaging Biometrics LLC, based in Elm Grove, Wisconsin, develops quantitative MRI and CT analysis software for disease evaluation and monitoring, distributed globally. Further information: www.imagingbiometrics.com. Kirkstall Limited, acquired in October 2025, develops the Quasi-Vivo® organ-on-a-chip platform for pharmaceutical research, toxicology and disease modelling, with distributors in the United States, China and South Korea. Further information: www.kirkstall.com.

company_30s.what_they_do: IBAI develops imaging‑biomarker software that maps tumor biology in real time, mainly for brain cancers like glioblastoma. The platform reads scans and turns them into actionable maps that clinicians can use to tailor treatment plans.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Precision imaging fills a critical gap in oncology: doctors still rely on generic scans that miss subtle changes. IBAI’s tools promise earlier detection, better monitoring of therapy response, and ultimately improved patient outcomes. The global market for advanced imaging in oncology is projected to grow into the tens of billions over the next decade, giving IBAI a large addressable space.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in a transition phase – it has moved from early‑stage trials into phase II studies and is now looking to commercialise its software. Partnerships with GE and Vivan Therapeutics indicate a strategy to scale distribution, while grant funding and a recent phase II data release show it is still developing the product pipeline. The share price sits very low, reflecting the company’s current cash‑burn profile.

rns_narrative: IBAI has just announced the commercialisation of its phase II results, signalling a move from clinical research to market readiness.
The company has secured a series of grants and a partnership with GE to expand global distribution, indicating a shift toward revenue generation.
Corporate updates show board changes and an AGM that highlighted strategic direction, suggesting the firm is aligning its operations for growth.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet anticipation – the community is largely silent but ready to react to the next move.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No clear bullish chatter; the absence of hype actually signals a contrarian opportunity.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Skeptics point to the still‑unproven clinical data and the risk of regulatory delays as reasons to stay away.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Initially skeptical, the mood has slightly warmed after the phase II announcement, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – the story is still buried, which maximises surprise potential when new information surfaces.

near_term_catalysts: Release of detailed phase II efficacy data – a positive report could unlock a pricing strategy.
Activation of the GE distribution channel – a sign that the product will reach a wider customer base.
Any regulatory filing or approval milestone – a green light would remove a major uncertainty.
A sudden spike in trading volume that breaks through the 30‑day low, indicating renewed institutional interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained volume increase, price crossing the 30‑day moving average, and any official statement from the FDA or EMA confirming the phase II findings.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is deep in a crash cycle – it has already fallen roughly 86% from its peak. APEX shows a tight compression, suggesting that the market is ready for a mean‑reversion bounce once the next catalyst arrives.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The deep crash (≈ 86% down) places the share in the high‑reward orange zone. If the price climbs to 50 % of its all‑time high – a modest half‑recovery – that represents roughly a 260 % upside from current levels. Past orange signals have delivered 112 % returns on average, so a bounce to mid‑range is plausible.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risks are a failure to meet phase II endpoints, regulatory setbacks, or a lack of traction from the GE partnership. If the product never gains market acceptance, the share could retreat back to the 14 % of peak level or lower.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: ~260 %. Potential downside: 14 % of peak (the current level). Asymmetry ratio ≈ 18 : 1 – a highly favourable risk‑reward profile for a contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a clear volume surge that confirms the 30‑day low is broken. Allocate 10 % of the portfolio to the position, place a stop 5 % below the entry to protect against a quick retracement, and set a target at the 50 % of all‑time high level.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the phase II data is released or the GE channel is activated. Watch the price for a sustained move above the 30‑day moving average and for a spike in volume as the most reliable confirmation.
playbook.if_passing: None identified – no imminent delisting or bankruptcy signals. The setup remains a viable contrarian opportunity.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep crash paired with an upcoming phase II data release creates a contrarian play with an 18‑to‑1 upside‑to‑downside asymmetry.
